subject: First Aid & Safety
title: How the Brain Responds to Oxygen Loss
description: A short lesson to help you apply How the Brain Responds to Oxygen Loss.

hook.text: Hold your breath for a minute. Now imagine your brain with no oxygen for four.

buildup.text: The brain consumes 20% of the body's oxygen despite being only 2% of its weight.

discovery.text: After 4 minutes without oxygen, brain cells begin to die permanently.

twist.text: Cold temperatures slow this process—drowning victims in icy water sometimes survive longer.

climax.text: CPR buys time by pushing oxygenated blood to the brain until help arrives.

punchline.text: Four minutes. That's all the brain gives you.

quiz.question: How much of the body's oxygen does the brain consume?
